scope: sharedmodule/llmswitch-core/src non-native production TypeScript from llmswitch-rustification-audit

classificationContract.native_shell_ok: TypeScript is a fail-fast native facade or type projection over Rust truth.
classificationContract.type_shell_ok: TypeScript is compile-time/public API shape only and owns no runtime decision.
classificationContract.ts_io_shell_ok: TypeScript owns unavoidable Node host IO, timers, filesystem, process, Map storage, or diagnostics emission only.
classificationContract.parser_io_ok: TypeScript parses native JSON strings and type-projects Rust-owned output only.
classificationContract.diagnostic_io_ok: TypeScript records or emits diagnostic observations only; no request/response/route/tool policy.
classificationContract.hardStopRule: Any file not listed here, or any listed file with an empty cannotShrinkFurtherBecause reason, is rustification debt.

entries.minimumTsRole: Provider response Node IO shell that reads streams, invokes Rust Hub Pipeline/effect planners, executes host side effects, and writes returned SSE frames.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: The remaining code touches Node Readable streams, MetadataCenter host objects, response store IO, usage persistence, servertool shell execution, stage recording, and returned SSE stream construction. Provider response parsing, governance, projection, effect planning, and error descriptors are Rust-owned through native calls.

entries.minimumTsRole: Type declarations for TS host/Rust bridge boundaries.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Current Node package compiles TS callers against these structural types; further removal requires a separate Rust-generated declaration pipeline, not Hub runtime migration.

entries.minimumTsRole: Attaches timing summaries to MetadataCenter debug snapshots and logs observations.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: It must touch JS MetadataCenter/debug snapshot objects and console IO in the current Node host; moving it requires replacing the diagnostics host surface.

entries.minimumTsRole: TS structural contract for host/native request and adapter context wiring.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Current TS host modules compile against these public shapes; Rust cannot remove compile-time TS declarations without a separate generated bindings system.

entries.minimumTsRole: Shared JSON structural type declarations for TS host boundaries.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: It is a TypeScript compile-time/public type surface for JS JSON values; Rust migration cannot replace TS type checking without generated declaration adoption.
These are TS compiler contracts for the Node host boundary; Rust owns runtime builders, but TS declarations remain until generated bindings replace them.

entries.minimumTsRole: Node Map/FS/global singleton/timer/error-projection shell executing Rust native plans.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: The remaining code owns Node-only persistence files, process-global singleton state, timers, Map indexes, and exception projection. Moving it further requires a separate Rust-backed persistent store plus Node host lifecycle replacement; no Hub continuation semantics remain in TS.

entries.minimumTsRole: Fail-fast native policy facade for provider failure decisions.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Node executor/provider shells call this facade; removal requires moving those host call sites or replacing them with generated bindings.

entries.minimumTsRole: Virtual Router public TS config/contract declaration surface.
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: VR runtime is Rust-only, but Node config/bootstrap/tests compile against these TS contract declarations. Removing them requires generated Rust schema declarations or a full config API migration.
It is host OS/path IO; moving it requires a separate runtime lifecycle Rust migration and does not close Hub/VR semantics.
Current TS servertool shells/tests compile against these types; Rust owns execution semantics but cannot eliminate TypeScript declarations without generated bindings or full shell removal.

entries.minimumTsRole: Minimal NoopStatsCenter facade for TS bridge consumers (state-integrations.ts getStatsCenter)
entries.cannotShrinkFurtherBecause: Bridge consumers require requireCoreDist("telemetry/stats-center").getStatsCenter() to return a StatsCenter-compatible object. Rust cannot replace this without cross-package bridge migration.
